pages.raw_text:
WASHINGTON. April, 4.-Sensa-
tional testimony was given by George
II. Maxwell, president of the National
Irrigation association, before the
house committee on irrigation today.
the purpose of his appearance being
to urge the repeal of the desert land
act. He was asked pointed questions
relating to the lobby for the repeal
bin, which is alleged to be maintained
by certain railroads. He denied its
existence, but admitted that his as
ISOLATION was working for the repeal
of the law


When asked how the association
was supported, he said by contribu-
tons from railroads and others He
named the following as paying $4,000
each annually: The Great Northern.
Northern Pacific, Union Pacific,
Southern Pacific. Burlington and San
Ia Fe. The Rock Island contributes
$4,000.


The association. he said. was or
ganized to help in the passage of the
original irrigation act and to keep up
campaign of education. The rail-
roads contributed because they ems
dorsed this policy. They had not been
sounded on the project of repeating
the desert land act.
